The proposed model, DTD, is a novel model for document image tampering detection.
The overall architecture of the DTD model is illustrated in Fig. 4.
The DTD model consists of modules such as Visual Perception Head to extract visual features from original images.
Frequency Perception Head converts DCT coefficients of images into frequency domain feature embeddings.
Visual Perception Head is used to extract visual features from input images.
DCT coefficients are used as input to the Frequency Perception Head to capture tampering clues in the frequency domain.
Images are captured by digital devices such as cameras and smart phones.
DCT coefficients' features are good at capturing the BAG's discontinuities caused by patching and compression.
Tampering on images causes discontinuities between tampered regions and authentic regions.
The structure of the proposed Frequency Perception Head follows a dual-head design.
The DCT coefficient map is embedded using a set of orthonormal basis to obtain features Fp1.
The Y-channel quantization table is extracted from the image I.
The quantization table is expanded to match the DCT coefficients.
The quantization table embeddings are multiplied with Fp1 to obtain features Fp2.
Fp1 and Fp2 are concatenated together and down-sampled to 8 x Cp3 using a convolution layer.
Each pixel of Fp3 represents an 8x8 block from the original DCT coefficients, aligning with the BAG of 8 x W.
The input to Frequency Perception Head takes DCT coefficients with the quantization table of the image I.
Position embedding is applied on Fp3 to enhance their position information for better alignment with visual features.
MoblieConv Layers are applied on Fp3 to obtain the frequency feature embedding Fd.
Features from frequency domain and visual domain are fused using a multi-modality Transformer.
The visual domain is processed by the multi-modality Transformer to incorporate visual perception and frequency perception outputs by a scSE module and convolution layer for dimension reduction.
The visual perception head's output Fv and Frequency Perception Head's output Fd are concatenated and incorporated together by a scSE module.
The output of the visual perception head is processed by a 1 × 1 convolution layer for dimension reduction.
The visual domain features are further processed by Swin Transformer blocks to extract higher-level multi-modality features.
The Multi-view Iterative Decoder is a new decoder framework proposed to make use of different features in sizes iteratively for predicting more accurate results.
The decoder features D0,n are calculated based on the encoder's output features Ff 0, Ff 1, Ff 2, Ff 3 by four cascaded iteration operations.
The concatenated decoder features D0,n are used to predict the final results through a convolution layer.
The convolution layer is used to get the final predictions in the context.
The prediction mask of an input image is compared to the ground-truth mask in the context.
The Cross-Entropy Loss is part of the loss function along with the ground-truth mask in the context.
